title: Product Manager at a Startup – Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Startup product manager responsibilities, required skills, and resume keywords. How to position your experience for fast-moving PM roles.
intro: Product managers at startups wear many hats: roadmap ownership, customer discovery, and working closely with engineering and design. This guide outlines typical responsibilities, skills, and resume keywords so you can present your experience in a way that resonates with startup hiring managers and ATS.
responsibilities: Define product vision, strategy, and roadmap in alignment with business goals
Gather and prioritize customer and stakeholder requirements
Write clear specs, user stories, and acceptance criteria for engineering
Work with design and engineering in agile cycles to ship features
Analyze usage data and feedback to iterate on product decisions
Communicate roadmap and trade-offs to leadership and cross-functional teams
requiredSkills: Data-informed decision making (metrics, A/B tests, user research)
Technical fluency to work with engineers on scope and feasibility
salaryRange: Varies widely; early-stage often $90,000–$140,000 base plus equity; growth-stage can be higher.
body:
Startup PM roles value ownership and outcomes over formal process. On your resume, lead with impact: features shipped, metrics improved, and how you influenced strategy or prioritization. Use concrete numbers (e.g. adoption rates, revenue impact, user growth).

Tailoring your resume to each startup's stage and product area will help you stand out. WadeCV can help you reframe your existing PM experience for startup-style job descriptions so your strongest wins are front and centre.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on process (e.g. 'ran agile') without business outcomes
Generic bullets that could apply to any company
Skipping metrics; startups care about growth and impact
interviewTips: Prepare product case studies and prioritisation frameworks
Be ready to discuss trade-offs and how you used data
Show you can work with limited resources and ambiguity

faq.question: What do startup PM hiring managers look for?
faq.answer: Evidence of ownership (you shipped X, it led to Y), comfort with ambiguity, and ability to work closely with engineering and design. Metrics and outcomes matter more than job title or company size.

title: Data Analyst in Healthcare – Job Description & Resume Tips
metaDescription: Healthcare data analyst responsibilities, skills, salary ranges, and resume keywords to land roles in providers, payers, and health tech.
intro: Data analysts in healthcare help organizations make sense of clinical, operational, and financial data. Roles exist at providers, payers, pharma, and health tech companies. This guide covers typical job requirements and how to tailor your resume for these positions.
responsibilities: Build and maintain dashboards and reports for clinical, operational, or financial metrics
Clean, transform, and analyze large datasets (claims, EMR, surveys)
Support ad hoc analyses and present findings to clinical and business stakeholders
Ensure data quality and compliance with HIPAA and internal policies
Collaborate with IT, clinical teams, and leadership to define reporting needs
requiredSkills: SQL and experience with relational databases and data warehouses
Visualization tools (e.g. Tableau, Power BI, Looker)
Statistical literacy and basic scripting (Python or R often preferred)
Understanding of healthcare data (claims, EMR, coding) and regulatory context
Ability to explain technical results to non-technical audiences
salaryRange: Often $65,000–$110,000 depending on experience, organization, and location.
body: Healthcare data roles value both technical skills and domain awareness. On your resume, highlight experience with healthcare datasets, compliance considerations, and how your analyses drove decisions for clinicians or operations. Quantify impact where you can (e.g. reports used by X teams, time saved, or improvements in key metrics). Tailoring your resume to each employer's focus—clinical quality, revenue cycle, population health, or product analytics—will improve your fit. Use WadeCV to align your background with specific healthcare data analyst job descriptions.

faq.question: What skills do healthcare data analyst roles look for?
faq.answer: SQL, visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI), and often Python or R. Domain knowledge of healthcare data (claims, EMR, HIPAA) and the ability to explain results to non-technical stakeholders are also important.

title: Customer Success Manager Job Description & Resume Keywords
metaDescription: Customer success manager responsibilities, skills, salary ranges, and resume keywords. How to tailor your resume for CSM roles in SaaS and tech.
intro: Customer success managers focus on adoption, retention, and growth of existing customers. The role is critical in SaaS and subscription businesses. This guide outlines typical job requirements and how to present your experience so recruiters and ATS recognise your fit.
responsibilities: Onboard new customers and drive time-to-value and adoption
Monitor health scores, usage, and risk indicators; run proactive outreach
Conduct business reviews and identify expansion or upsell opportunities
Collaborate with support, product, and sales to resolve issues and share feedback
Renew contracts and reduce churn through relationship and value delivery
Document processes and contribute to playbooks and best practices
requiredSkills: Experience with CRM (e.g. Salesforce), support tools, and product usage data
Ability to understand customer goals and map solutions to outcomes
Organisation and project management across multiple accounts
Empathy and resilience when handling escalations
salaryRange: Often $70,000–$120,000 base plus variable compensation, depending on segment and company.
body: CSM roles reward outcomes: retention rates, expansion revenue, and improved adoption or NPS. On your resume, lead with metrics—renewal rates, revenue retained or expanded, number of accounts, and feedback scores. Show that you can own the relationship and work across internal teams. Tailoring your resume to the company's segment (SMB, mid-market, enterprise) and industry will help. WadeCV can help you align your customer-facing experience with CSM job descriptions and highlight your strongest impact.

faq.question: What metrics should I put on a customer success manager resume?
faq.answer: Renewal rate, retention, NPS or CSAT, expansion revenue, number of accounts managed, and adoption or usage metrics. Lead with outcomes that show you drove retention and growth.

commonMistakes: Vague bullets without metrics
Skipping renewal or retention numbers
No evidence of cross-functional work

faq.question: How many technical details should be on a data scientist resume?

No automation or tool depth

title: Project Manager – Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Project manager job description, core skills, and resume keywords. How to write a PM resume that passes ATS and impresses hiring managers in 2026.
intro: Project managers plan, execute, and close projects on time and within budget while coordinating teams and stakeholders. This guide covers the responsibilities, skills, and resume keywords you need to land a PM role across industries — from tech to construction to healthcare.
responsibilities: Define project scope, goals, deliverables, and timelines
Develop and maintain project plans, schedules, and risk logs
Coordinate cross-functional teams and manage stakeholder communication
Track project progress against milestones and adjust plans as needed
Manage project budgets, resource allocation, and vendor relationships
Run retrospectives and document lessons learned
requiredSkills: Project planning and scheduling (MS Project, Asana, Jira, Monday.com)
salaryRange: $75,000–$130,000 depending on industry, company size, and PMP certification.
body: PMP certification and proven delivery track record are the two biggest differentiators on a PM resume. Lead every bullet with an outcome: on time, under budget, reduced risk, improved delivery velocity. Quantify scope (team size, budget managed, number of concurrent projects). Tailor tool mentions to the job description. WadeCV can help you surface the strongest delivery stories from your experience and frame them for each PM role.
commonMistakes: Generic bullets like 'managed projects' with no scale or outcome
Not quantifying budget or team size
Omitting certification (PMP, PRINCE2, PMI-ACP) if you have one
interviewTips: Use STAR format for behavioral questions about handling scope creep or missed deadlines
Be ready to walk through a full project lifecycle end-to-end
Show a specific example of a risk you caught and mitigated before it became an issue

faq.question: Do I need a PMP certification to get a project manager job?
faq.answer: No, but it helps — especially for mid-to-senior roles or government contracts. Many employers require or prefer it. If you don't have it, emphasise years of experience and concrete delivery outcomes.

title: Business Analyst – Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Business analyst job description, key skills, and resume keywords. How to write a BA resume that gets past ATS and shows your analytical and stakeholder skills.
intro: Business analysts bridge the gap between business needs and technical solutions. They gather requirements, model processes, analyse data, and translate findings into actionable recommendations. This guide covers the BA role across IT, finance, and operations contexts.
responsibilities: Elicit, document, and validate business requirements from stakeholders
Model current and future-state business processes (flowcharts, swim lanes, UML)
Conduct gap analysis and feasibility assessments
Translate requirements into functional specs, user stories, or business rules
Coordinate with development teams to ensure requirements are implemented correctly
Analyse data to identify trends, root causes, and improvement opportunities
Create dashboards, reports, and presentations for leadership
requiredSkills: Requirements elicitation and documentation (BRD, FRD, user stories)
Process mapping and modelling (Visio, Lucidchart, BPMN)
Data analysis (Excel, SQL, Tableau, Power BI)
Understanding of SDLC and Agile delivery
salaryRange: $70,000–$110,000 depending on industry and seniority; finance and tech BAs often earn more.
body: The strongest BA resumes show impact at both ends: requirements clarity (prevented rework, reduced ambiguity) and outcomes (system shipped, process improved, cost saved). Include specific tools (SQL, Power BI, Visio) and methodology experience. CBAP or CCBA certification is a differentiator for senior roles. WadeCV can tailor your BA experience to the specific domain and tools in each job description.
commonMistakes: Only listing activities ('gathered requirements') without outcomes
Missing tools — recruiters scan for SQL, Tableau, Visio
No mention of domain expertise (finance, healthcare, retail)
interviewTips: Prepare a process improvement case study showing before/after metrics
Be ready to walk through how you handle conflicting stakeholder priorities
Show examples of requirements you wrote and how they guided development

faq.question: What is the difference between a business analyst and a data analyst?
faq.answer: Business analysts focus on process improvement and bridging business needs to technical solutions. Data analysts focus on extracting and interpreting quantitative data. Many roles overlap, especially where SQL and dashboards are involved.

title: Operations Manager – Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Operations manager responsibilities, skills, and resume keywords for 2026. Write a compelling ops manager resume that shows efficiency gains and team leadership.
intro: Operations managers keep organisations running efficiently. Whether in logistics, manufacturing, retail, or tech, they manage people, processes, and resources to deliver results on time and within budget. This guide covers the core responsibilities, skills, and resume strategies for landing an ops manager role.
responsibilities: Oversee day-to-day operations across functions such as supply chain, logistics, or service delivery
Develop and implement operational processes, policies, and SOPs
Lead, coach, and performance-manage frontline and supervisory teams
Monitor KPIs and operational metrics; identify and resolve bottlenecks
Manage vendor relationships, procurement, and contracts
Drive continuous improvement initiatives (Lean, Six Sigma, Kaizen)
Manage operational budgets and control costs
salaryRange: $70,000–$120,000 depending on industry and scope; manufacturing and logistics ops managers often earn $90,000+.
body: Ops manager resumes should lead with numbers: cost savings ($X saved), efficiency gains (cycle time reduced by Y%), and team scale (led N-person team). Frame process improvements as before/after with business impact. Lean or Six Sigma certification adds weight. WadeCV can help you extract and emphasise the operational wins from your experience for each specific role.
commonMistakes: Generic bullets about 'managing operations' without scale or outcome
No mention of specific industries or operational domains
Omitting metrics — ops roles are defined by measurable improvement
interviewTips: Prepare a continuous improvement project with before/after metrics
Be ready to discuss how you've handled operational crises or under-performing teams
Show how you balance short-term firefighting with long-term process improvement

faq.question: Do operations managers need a specific degree?
faq.answer: Most operations manager roles require a bachelor's degree, ideally in business, operations management, or a related field. MBA and Six Sigma certifications are valued for senior roles. Practical experience and quantified results often matter more than the specific degree.

title: Cybersecurity Analyst – Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Cybersecurity analyst job description, key skills, and resume keywords for 2026. How to write an infosec resume that highlights technical skills and incident response experience.
intro: Cybersecurity analysts protect organisations from threats by monitoring systems, detecting incidents, and responding to breaches. Demand is high and growing — but so is competition. This guide covers the responsibilities, skills, and resume strategies to land a cybersecurity analyst role.
responsibilities: Monitor security information and event management (SIEM) systems for threats
Investigate and respond to security incidents and alerts
Conduct vulnerability assessments and penetration testing
Implement and maintain security controls, firewalls, and endpoint protection
Write incident reports and recommend remediation steps
Stay current on threat intelligence and emerging attack vectors
Collaborate with IT and development teams on security best practices
requiredSkills: SIEM platforms (Splunk, Microsoft Sentinel, IBM QRadar)
Security certifications: CompTIA Security+, CEH, CISSP
salaryRange: $75,000–$130,000; senior analysts and those with CISSP can earn $130,000+.
body: Cybersecurity resumes must show both technical depth and real-world incident experience. List specific tools and platforms, certifications, and — where possible — incidents you helped contain or risks you reduced. Government and regulated-industry roles often require clearances, so note those if applicable. WadeCV can align your security experience with each job's specific stack and threat environment.
commonMistakes: Generic security language without specific tools or platforms
No mention of certifications or active study towards them
Missing metrics — e.g. alerts triaged per day, MTTD/MTTR improvements
interviewTips: Be ready to walk through an incident response scenario end-to-end
Know the MITRE ATT&CK framework and be able to map threats to tactics
Discuss a specific vulnerability you found and how it was remediated

faq.question: Is CompTIA Security+ enough to get a cybersecurity analyst job?
faq.answer: Security+ is a widely recognised entry-level certification and a solid starting point. Many junior SOC and security analyst roles require or prefer it. For mid-level roles, CEH or CISSP (or working towards them) adds credibility.

title: Machine Learning Engineer – Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Machine learning engineer job description, skills, and resume keywords. How to write an ML engineer resume that showcases models shipped, tools used, and measurable impact.
intro: Machine learning engineers build, train, and deploy ML models at production scale. They sit between data science and software engineering, turning research into reliable, scalable systems. This guide covers the core responsibilities, skills, and resume strategies for landing an ML engineer role.
responsibilities: Design, develop, and train machine learning models for classification, regression, NLP, or computer vision tasks
Build data pipelines for model training, validation, and serving
Deploy models to production and maintain serving infrastructure
Monitor model performance, detect drift, and retrain as needed
Collaborate with data scientists to productionise research code
Optimise models for latency, throughput, and cost
Write unit tests and documentation for ML systems
requiredSkills: ML fundamentals: supervised/unsupervised learning, deep learning, NLP
Data pipelines: Apache Spark, Airflow, dbt
Model deployment: Docker, Kubernetes, MLflow, SageMaker
SQL and cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure)
salaryRange: $130,000–$200,000+; top-tier tech companies and AI labs pay above $200,000 with equity.
body: ML engineer resumes must demonstrate both modelling depth and engineering rigour. Lead bullets with models shipped, business metrics improved, and scale (requests/second, dataset size). Mention open-source contributions or Kaggle rankings if relevant. Show production deployments, not just notebooks. WadeCV can help you translate your ML work into the language each job description prioritises.
commonMistakes: Research-only bullets with no production deployment evidence
Listing frameworks without noting what you built with them
No mention of scale, latency, or business impact of deployed models
interviewTips: Be ready for a system design interview — ML serving pipelines, feature stores, monitoring
Know bias/variance tradeoff, regularisation, and when to use which model family
Prepare a case study of a model you trained, deployed, and monitored in production

faq.question: What is the difference between a data scientist and a machine learning engineer?
faq.answer: Data scientists focus on exploration, analysis, and building models in notebooks. ML engineers focus on productionising those models — scalable infrastructure, serving systems, monitoring, and reliability. Roles overlap significantly, especially at smaller companies.

title: Cloud Engineer – Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Cloud engineer job description, skills, and resume keywords for AWS, Azure, and GCP roles. How to write a cloud infrastructure resume that highlights architecture and cost optimisation.
intro: Cloud engineers design, build, and manage cloud infrastructure on AWS, Azure, or GCP. They bridge architecture and operations — automating provisioning, ensuring reliability, and optimising cost. This guide covers responsibilities, skills, and resume strategies for cloud engineering roles.
responsibilities: Design and implement scalable, secure cloud architectures on AWS, Azure, or GCP
Provision and manage infrastructure using IaC tools (Terraform, CloudFormation, Pulumi)
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines and deployment automation
Monitor infrastructure health, performance, and cost; set up alerting and on-call runbooks
Implement cloud security controls: IAM, VPC, encryption, secrets management
Migrate on-premises workloads to the cloud
Collaborate with development teams on cloud-native architecture
requiredSkills: Cloud platforms: AWS, Azure, or GCP (solutions architect level)
Infrastructure as Code: Terraform, CloudFormation, or Pulumi
Networking: VPC, subnets, load balancers, DNS, CDN
Security: IAM, RBAC, secrets management, compliance frameworks
salaryRange: $110,000–$175,000; senior cloud architects earn $180,000+.
body: Cloud engineering resumes should show both breadth (multi-service, multi-cloud awareness) and depth (you can own a production system end-to-end). Quantify reliability (uptime %, incident reduction), cost savings ($X/month reduced), and scale (requests/day, data volume). Include cloud certifications (AWS SAA, CKA, Azure AZ-104) prominently. WadeCV can tailor your cloud experience to each employer's specific stack and cloud provider.
commonMistakes: Listing cloud services without showing what you built or achieved
No cost or reliability metrics
Omitting certifications or active pursuit of them
interviewTips: Prepare for a cloud architecture design exercise (e.g. design a highly available web app)
Know your cloud billing model and how to optimise reserved instances vs spot
Walk through a production incident you diagnosed and resolved

faq.question: Which cloud certification is best for a cloud engineer?
faq.answer: AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate (SAA-C03) is the most widely recognised. Azure AZ-104 and Google Professional Cloud Architect are strong for their respective platforms. Kubernetes CKA is valuable for cloud-native roles regardless of cloud provider.

title: Content Writer – Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Content writer job description, skills, and resume keywords. How to write a content writing resume that showcases SEO expertise, editorial range, and measurable content results.
intro: Content writers create blog posts, articles, web copy, case studies, and other written materials that attract, educate, and convert audiences. Modern content roles increasingly require SEO knowledge, analytics fluency, and the ability to work with AI writing tools. This guide covers what employers are looking for and how to position your experience.
responsibilities: Write, edit, and publish blog posts, articles, landing pages, and web copy
Conduct keyword research and optimise content for search intent
Interview subject matter experts and translate technical topics for non-expert audiences
Manage an editorial calendar and meet publishing deadlines
Analyse content performance (organic traffic, time on page, conversion rate) and iterate
Write and optimise email newsletters and social media content
Collaborate with design, SEO, and marketing teams on campaigns
requiredSkills: Long-form and short-form writing across multiple formats
SEO fundamentals: keyword research, on-page optimisation, internal linking
AI writing tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Jasper) — knowing how to use and edit AI output
Strong grammar, style, and adaptability to different brand voices
salaryRange: $45,000–$80,000 for in-house roles; senior and specialist content strategists earn $80,000–$110,000.
body: Content writer resumes should showcase results, not just output. Pair every piece of writing experience with a metric: organic traffic generated, ranking positions achieved, leads attributed to content, or engagement rates. Include links to a portfolio or specific published pieces. Showing SEO and analytics fluency is increasingly essential. WadeCV can tailor your content experience to each employer's domain and content type.
commonMistakes: No metrics — 'wrote blog posts' is not enough
No portfolio link or examples of published work
Missing SEO skills or tool mentions if you have them
interviewTips: Bring 2–3 examples of content you're proud of with their results
Be ready to do a short writing or editing exercise
Show how you approach a new topic you know nothing about

faq.question: Does a content writer need to know SEO?
faq.answer: In most in-house and agency roles, yes. Even if there's a dedicated SEO team, content writers are expected to understand keyword intent, on-page basics, and how to structure content for both readers and search engines. It's a table-stakes skill in 2026.

title: Social Media Manager – Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Social media manager job description, skills, and resume keywords. Write a social media resume that shows platform expertise, audience growth, and content strategy results.
intro: Social media managers build and manage a brand's presence across platforms like LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, and X. The role combines content creation, community management, analytics, and increasingly, paid social. This guide covers what employers look for and how to frame your experience.
responsibilities: Develop and execute a social media content strategy across platforms
Create, schedule, and publish content (copy, images, short-form video)
Manage community engagement: respond to comments, DMs, and mentions
Track and report on social performance metrics (reach, engagement, follower growth, CTR)
Run and optimise paid social campaigns (Meta Ads, LinkedIn Ads, TikTok Ads)
Monitor brand mentions and social listening trends
Collaborate with design, content, and product teams on campaigns
requiredSkills: Platform expertise: LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, X, Facebook
Content creation: copywriting, basic graphic design (Canva, Adobe), short-form video
Scheduling tools: Buffer, Hootsuite, Later, Sprout Social
Analytics: native platform analytics, Google Analytics, Looker Studio
Paid social: Meta Ads Manager, LinkedIn Campaign Manager
Community management and brand voice consistency
salaryRange: $45,000–$80,000 for in-house roles; agencies and senior roles can reach $90,000.
body: Social media resumes must show platform results: follower growth percentages, engagement rates, reach milestones, and ROAS on paid campaigns. Include specific platforms you've managed and the audience size. Show creative range — written content, video, campaigns — and analytics fluency. WadeCV can tailor your social experience to each employer's platform mix and industry.
commonMistakes: No metrics on follower growth, engagement, or reach
Not specifying which platforms you managed and at what scale
Missing paid social experience if you have it
interviewTips: Bring examples of organic content that outperformed expectations
Be ready to audit their current social presence and suggest improvements
Show how you balance brand voice with platform-specific trends

faq.question: What metrics matter most on a social media manager resume?
faq.answer: Engagement rate (likes/comments/shares vs reach), follower growth (% or absolute), reach or impressions at scale, and ROAS on paid campaigns. Pick the 2–3 most impressive numbers for each role and lead with them.

title: Supply Chain Analyst – Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Supply chain analyst job description, skills, and resume keywords. Write a supply chain resume that highlights data analysis, forecasting, and operational improvements.
intro: Supply chain analysts optimise the flow of goods, information, and resources from suppliers to customers. They use data to improve forecasting accuracy, reduce costs, and increase resilience. This guide covers the responsibilities, skills, and resume strategies for supply chain analyst roles in manufacturing, retail, and tech.
responsibilities: Analyse supply chain data to identify cost reduction and efficiency opportunities
Build and maintain demand forecasting models
Monitor inventory levels, reorder points, and safety stock
Work with procurement, logistics, and operations teams to resolve supply issues
Prepare supply chain performance reports and KPI dashboards
Evaluate and qualify suppliers; support contract negotiations
Support S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning) processes
requiredSkills: Data analysis: Excel (advanced), SQL, Power BI, Tableau
Inventory management and warehouse management systems
salaryRange: $55,000–$95,000; senior analysts and supply chain managers earn $90,000–$120,000.
careerPath: Director of Supply Chain / VP Operations
body: Supply chain analyst resumes should quantify impact: forecast accuracy improvement (% error reduction), inventory reduction ($X or % turns improved), cost savings from supplier negotiation, or lead time reductions. Show analytical tool depth (SQL, Power BI, SAP) alongside domain knowledge. WadeCV can tailor your supply chain experience to each employer's industry and ERP environment.
commonMistakes: No quantification of forecast accuracy or inventory metrics
Not specifying ERP or analytics tools used
Generic bullets about 'analysing supply chain data'
interviewTips: Prepare a forecast accuracy improvement case study
Be ready to discuss how you handled a supply disruption or shortage
Show your understanding of the full end-to-end supply chain for their industry

faq.question: What certifications help a supply chain analyst stand out?
faq.answer: APICS CSCP (Certified Supply Chain Professional) and CPIM (Certified in Production and Inventory Management) are the most recognised. For data-heavy roles, SQL and Power BI certifications add value.

title: Product Designer – Job Description & Resume Guide
metaDescription: Product designer job description, skills, and resume keywords. How to write a UX/product design resume that showcases your design process, outcomes, and portfolio.
intro: Product designers shape the end-to-end user experience of digital products — from research and wireframing to high-fidelity prototypes and design systems. The role combines UX thinking with visual design skills and close collaboration with product and engineering teams. This guide covers what employers look for and how to position your portfolio.
responsibilities: Lead user research: interviews, usability tests, surveys, and journey mapping
Create wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity designs in Figma or Sketch
Define and maintain design systems and component libraries
Collaborate with product managers to shape requirements and prioritisation
Work with engineers on implementation; conduct design QA
Iterate on designs based on user feedback and product analytics
Contribute to design critique and cross-team design reviews
requiredSkills: Figma (primary tool for most teams), Sketch, or Adobe XD
User research and usability testing methods
Collaboration with product and engineering (Agile ceremonies)
salaryRange: $90,000–$160,000 depending on seniority and company; FAANG/unicorn product designers earn more.
body: Product design resumes live and die by the portfolio. Every bullet should connect to a project the interviewer can review. Show design process (research → wireframe → test → iterate) and outcomes (engagement rates, task completion, satisfaction scores). Quantify where you can. WadeCV can tailor your design experience descriptions to each role's emphasis — research-heavy, systems-focused, or consumer-facing product.
commonMistakes: No portfolio link or link to a locked/broken portfolio
Tool-listing without showing process or outcomes
No mention of design QA, handoff, or collaboration with engineers
interviewTips: Prepare a case study walkthrough: problem → research → design decisions → outcome
Be ready to critique your own designs and discuss what you'd change
Show how you handle feedback from stakeholders who disagree with your design choices

faq.question: What is the difference between a UX designer and a product designer?
faq.answer: UX designers focus primarily on the user experience flow, research, and wireframing. Product designers take a broader scope — owning the full design from research through to high-fidelity UI, design systems, and working closely with product and engineering. In practice, the titles are often used interchangeably at many companies.
